---
title: "Touchpad working bad after session log out"
date: 2007-02-28
forum: Hardware &amp; Laptops
---

### Post by linuxmad on 2007-02-28
Hi,
I am using Edgy on a toshiba satellite m70-122, and everything was ok until a few days ago i noticed that when i logged off or suspended the touchpad gets very slow and the tap doesn't work. If I reboot it is fine. Log in=ok Logout=very slow. 
Tried restarting X but problem is still here. 
My xorg.conf:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

Section "Extensions"
      Option "Composite" "Enable"
  EndSection

As anyone else having this? Solutions? Was X or any related files updated lately? I guess so, but can't remember dates and what was updated.
Thanks for your help
José

---

### Post by linuxmad on 2007-03-01
It is fixed. Last update of xserver-xorg-input-synaptics was the one to blame for. I had to downgrade. It is FIXED!!!

---

### Post by sohaibma on 2007-03-06
i'm facing the same problem.
i'm a newb, so can u please explain me how to downgrade?

---

### Post by linuxmad on 2007-03-06
> i'm facing the same problem.
i'm a newb, so can u please explain me how to downgrade?

open synaptic.. search for xserver-xorg-input-synaptics.. after that select on the right screen of synaptic the package found (click once on it), the go to top menu package and select force version. A window will pop up where you will see the package installed. Click it and the previous version will show up. Select it and click force version. After that click apply and it will downgrade.
hope I helped:)

---
